revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kameyama_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{神社| 名称=亀山神社| 所在地=広島県呉市清水1丁目9-36| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|29|N|132|34|08|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=品陀和気命
帯中日子命
息長帯日売命| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）| 例祭=[[10月10日]]}} '''亀山神社'''（かめやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[呉市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧呉市内の総[[氏神]]として信仰される。 [[八幡神]]として品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）・帯中日子命（[[仲哀天皇]]）・息長帯日売命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== [[筑後国]][[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]に鎮座していた八幡神が、[[豊後国]]姫島、[[安芸国]]栃原を経て[[大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）8月15日に宮原村亀山（入船山）に遷座したのに始まると伝える。当社は皇城宮・大屋比売神社・大帯比売神社・比売志麻神社・鈴音宮などと呼ばれたが、一般には「八幡様」と呼ばれていた。[[明治]]19年（[[1886年]]）、亀山に海軍鎮守府を開設するのに伴い現在地に遷座した。 昭和16年（[[1941年]]）に県社に列格した。昭和32年（[[1957年]]）に別表神社に加列された。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kameyama-jinja.com 亀山神社]（公式サイト） [[Category:広島県の神社|かめやましんしや]] [[Category:府県社|かめやましんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|かめやましんしや]] [[Category:八幡宮|かめやましんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=亀山神社| 所在地=広島県呉市清水1丁目9-36| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|29|N|132|34|08|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=品陀和気命
帯中日子命
息長帯日売命| 社格=県社・別表神社| 創建=[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）| 例祭=[[10月10日]]}} '''亀山神社'''（かめやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[呉市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧呉市内の総[[氏神]]として信仰される。 [[八幡神]]として品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）・帯中日子命（[[仲哀天皇]]）・息長帯日売命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== [[筑後国]][[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]に鎮座していた八幡神が、[[豊後国]]姫島、[[安芸国]]栃原を経て[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）8月15日に宮原村亀山（入船山）に遷座したのに始まると伝える。当社は皇城宮・大屋比売神社・大帯比売神社・比売志麻神社・鈴音宮などと呼ばれたが、一般には「八幡様」と呼ばれていた。[[明治]]19年（[[1886年]]）、亀山に海軍鎮守府を開設するのに伴い現在地に遷座した。 [[昭和]]16年（[[1941年]]）に県社に列格した。昭和32年（[[1957年]]）に別表神社に加列された。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kameyama-jinja.com 亀山神社]（公式サイト） [[Category:広島県の神社|かめやましんしや]] [[Category:府県社|かめやましんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|かめやましんしや]] [[Category:八幡宮|かめやましんしや]]

息長帯日売命 |社格 = 旧県社・別表神社 |創建 = [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2日曜日を含む土・日曜日 |神事 = }} '''亀山神社'''（かめやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[呉市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧呉市内の総[[氏神]]として信仰される。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]として品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）・帯中日子命（[[仲哀天皇]]）・息長帯日売命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== [[筑後国]][[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]に鎮座していた八幡神が、[[豊後国]]姫島、[[安芸国]]栃原を経て[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）8月15日に宮原村亀山（入船山）に遷座したのに始まると伝える。当社は皇城宮・大屋比売神社・大帯比売神社・比売志麻神社・鈴音宮などと呼ばれたが、一般には「八幡様」と呼ばれていた。[[明治]]19年（[[1886年]]）、亀山に[[呉鎮守府]]を開設するのに伴い現在地に遷座した。 [[昭和]]16年（[[1941年]]）に県社に列格した。昭和32年（[[1957年]]）に別表神社に加列された。 == 関連項目 == * [[入船山公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kameyama-jinja.com 亀山神社]（神社公式） {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:呉市]] [[Category:呉市の歴史]] [[Category:安芸国]]

[[ja:亀山神社]] {{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:07 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称={{ill|Kameyama Shrine|ja|亀山神社}} | 画像=[[ファイル:亀山神社 社殿.JPG|180px]]
社殿 | 所在地={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}}清水1丁目9-36 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | 祭神={{ill|Hachiman|ja|応神天皇}}（{{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命}}）
{{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}（{{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命}}）
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}（{{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命}}） | 社格=旧{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} | 創建={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝}}3年（{{ill|703|ja|703年}}） | 本殿={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | 別名=八幡様 | 札所等= | 例祭=10月第2日曜日を含む土・日曜日 | 神事= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (かめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). ファイル:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]]

It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). ファイル:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}. The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]]

{{神社 |名称 = 亀山神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:亀山神社 社殿.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[広島県]][[呉市]]清水1丁目9-36 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|品陀和気命]]
[[仲哀天皇|帯中日子命]]
[[神功皇后|息長帯日売命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 八幡様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2日曜日を含む土・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=亀山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E}}}} }} '''亀山神社'''（かめやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[呉市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧呉市内の総[[氏神]]として信仰される。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]として品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）・帯中日子命（[[仲哀天皇]]）・息長帯日売命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ファイル:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == 歴史 == [[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]に鎮座していた八幡神が、[[豊後国]][[姫島 (大分県)|姫島]]、[[安芸国]]栃原を経て[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）8月15日に宮原村亀山（入船山）に遷座したのに始まると伝える。当社は皇城宮・大屋比売神社・大帯比売神社・比売志麻神社・鈴音宮などと呼ばれたが、一般には「八幡様」と呼ばれていた。[[明治]]19年（[[1886年]]）、亀山に[[呉鎮守府]]を開設するのに伴い現在地に遷座した。 [[昭和]]16年（[[1941年]]）に県社に列格した。昭和32年（[[1957年]]）に別表神社に加列された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[入船山公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kameyama-jinja.com 亀山神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja 亀山神社フェイスブック]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:呉市の建築物]] [[Category:呉市の歴史]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. {{Unreferenced|date=2017-8-28}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name={{ill|Kameyama Shrine|ja|亀山神社}} | image=[[File:亀山神社 社殿.JPG|180px]]
Honden | location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}} Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity={{ill|Hachiman|ja|応神天皇}} ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命}})
{{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命}})
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703年}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社[[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]]

It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]]

The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kameyama Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703年}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社[[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted Completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703年}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社[[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:completion tier 6]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]]\[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:New]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year }} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year }} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]]\[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]][[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year }} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year }} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

{{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|12=draft}})

{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝|12=draft}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703rd year|12=draft}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度|12=draft}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=draft}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|12=draft}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|12=draft}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|12=draft}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|12=draft}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代rank制度|12=draft|12=draft}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=draft|12=draft}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|12=draft|12=draft}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}). It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|12=draft|12=draft}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|12=draft|12=draft}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=draft|12=draft}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|12=draft|12=draft}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=draft|12=draft}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|12=draft|12=draft}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称 = 亀山神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:亀山神社 社殿.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[広島県]][[呉市]]清水1丁目9-36 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} |祭神 = [[応神天皇|品陀和気命]]
[[仲哀天皇|帯中日子命]]
[[神功皇后|息長帯日売命]] |社格 = 旧[[県社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 八幡様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2日曜日を含む土・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=亀山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E}}}} }} '''亀山神社'''（かめやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[呉市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧呉市内の総[[氏神]]として信仰される。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]として品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）・帯中日子命（[[仲哀天皇]]）・息長帯日売命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ファイル:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == 歴史 == [[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]に鎮座していた八幡神が、[[豊後国]][[姫島 (大分県)|姫島]]、[[安芸国]]栃原を経て[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）8月15日に宮原村亀山（入船山）に遷座したのに始まると伝える。当社は皇城宮・大屋比売神社・大帯比売神社・比売志麻神社・鈴音宮などと呼ばれたが、一般には「八幡様」と呼ばれていた。[[明治]]19年（[[1886年]]）、亀山に[[呉鎮守府]]を開設するのに伴い現在地に遷座した。 [[昭和]]16年（[[1941年]]）に県社に列格した。昭和32年（[[1957年]]）に別表神社に加列された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[入船山公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kameyama-jinja.com 亀山神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja 亀山神社フェイスブック]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:呉市の建築物]] [[Category:呉市の歴史]]

[[別表神社]] |創建 = （伝）[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]） |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 八幡様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2日曜日を含む土・日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=亀山神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E}}}} }} '''亀山神社'''（かめやまじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[呉市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧呉市内の総[[氏神]]として信仰される。 == 祭神 == [[八幡神]]として品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）・帯中日子命（[[仲哀天皇]]）・息長帯日売命（[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ファイル:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == 歴史 == [[豊前国]][[宇佐神宮|宇佐]]に鎮座していた八幡神が、[[豊後国]][[姫島 (大分県)|姫島]]、[[安芸国]]栃原を経て[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]3年（[[703年]]）8月15日に宮原村亀山（入船山）に遷座したのに始まると伝える。当社は皇城宮・大屋比売神社・大帯比売神社・比売志麻神社・鈴音宮などと呼ばれたが、一般には「八幡様」と呼ばれていた。[[明治]]19年（[[1886年]]）、亀山に[[呉鎮守府]]を開設するのに伴い現在地に遷座した。 [[昭和]]16年（[[1941年]]）に県社に列格した。昭和32年（[[1957年]]）に別表神社に加列された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[入船山公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.kameyama-jinja.com 亀山神社]（神社公式） *[http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja 亀山神社フェイスブック]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:かめやましんしや}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:呉市の建築物]] [[Category:呉市の歴史]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2017-8-28}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name={{ill|Kameyama Shrine|ja|亀山神社}} | image=[[File:亀山神社 社殿.JPG|180px]]
Honden | location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}{{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}} Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity={{ill|Hachiman|ja|応神天皇}} ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命}})
{{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命}})
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703rd year}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Honden | location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity={{ill|Hachiman|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|かめやましんしや}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 19|qq=}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 16|qq=}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 32|qq=}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

{{Unreferenced|date=2017-8-28}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name={{ill|Kameyama Shrine|ja|亀山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameyama Shrine|qq=}} | image=[[File:亀山神社 社殿.JPG|180px]]
Honden | location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kure|qq=}} Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity={{ill|Hachiman|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}} ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawakeno Mikoto|qq=}})
{{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai|qq=}} ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihomimi no Mikoto|qq=}})
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|qq=}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō 3|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/703|qq=}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kure|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=}} classification was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Province|qq=}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province|qq=}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aki Province|qq=}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 19|qq=}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 16|qq=}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 32|qq=}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Province|qq=}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province|qq=}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aki Province|qq=}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 19|qq=}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 16|qq=}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 32|qq=}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

{{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} | established={{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō 3|qq=}} 3 ({{ill|703|ja|703年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/703|qq=}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kure|ja|呉市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kure|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|qq=}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Jingū|lt=Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Province|qq=}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province|qq=}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in {{ill|Aki Province|ja|安芸国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aki Province|qq=}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 19|qq=}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 16|qq=}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 32|qq=}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=2017-8-28}}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name={{ill|Kameyama Shrine|ja|亀山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameyama Shrine|qq=}} | image=[[File:亀山神社 社殿.JPG|180px]]
Honden | location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]][[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]] Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hachiman]] ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawakeno Mikoto|qq=}})
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihomimi no Mikoto|qq=}})
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|qq=}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 19|qq=}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 16|qq=}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 32|qq=}} (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[en:Kameyama Shrine]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:History of Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]]

The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 16|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1941年}} (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 32|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1957年}} (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:History of Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]]
