[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|Meiji 19|ja|明治19年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 19|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1886年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|Shōwa 16|ja|昭和16年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 16|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1941年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1941), and in {{ill|Shōwa 32|ja|昭和32年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa 32|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1957年}}[[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:History of Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure]] [[Category:History of Kure]] [[Category:Kameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:History of Kure, Hiroshima]] [[Category:広島県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]]

Honden | location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]][[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]] Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hachiman]] ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawakeno Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}})
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihomimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇}})
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]][[Category:Years needing redirect replacements]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]]

The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]]

Honden | location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]][[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]] Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hachiman]] ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawakeno Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}})
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihomimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇}})
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]]

[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kameyama Shrine]] [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[sn:ameyama Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}}

[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|qq=|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|qq=|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|qq=|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}}

File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}}

The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Honden | location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]][[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]] Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hachiman]] ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}})
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|1=Chūai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q179971}})
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]] ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|1=Chūai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q179971}})
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Honden | location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]][[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]] Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity=[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hachiman]] ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}})

[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | established=[[:en:Taiho|Taihō 3]] 3 ([[:en:703|703]]) | honden=[[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kure, Hiroshima|Kure]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is revered as the general [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]], namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ([[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] in [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]]. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]], and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]], before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In [[:en:1886|Meiji 19]] (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in [[:en:1941|Showa 16]] (1941), and in [[:en:1957|Showa 32]] (1957), it was designated as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}). In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}} (1941), and in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=Showa 32}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} | established={{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō 3}} 3 ({{ill|703|en|703|WD=Q23500|lt=703}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kure, Hiroshima|en|Kure, Hiroshima|WD=Q223439|lt=Kure}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|WD=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}} (1941), and in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=Showa 32}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} | established={{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō 3}} 3 ({{ill|703|en|703|WD=Q23500|lt=703}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kure, Hiroshima|en|Kure, Hiroshima|WD=Q223439|lt=Kure}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|WD=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Himeshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Togihara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16}} (1941), and in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=Showa 32}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Honden | location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}{{ill|Kure, Hiroshima|en|Kure, Hiroshima|WD=Q223439|lt=Kure|1=Kure, Hiroshima}} Shimizu 1-9-36 | coordinates={{Coord|34|14|29.3|N|132|34|08.8|E|region:JP-34_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=亀山神社}} | deity={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman|1=Emperor Ōjin}} ({{ill|Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawakeno Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}})
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} ({{ill|Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja|帯中日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshihomimi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仲哀天皇|1=Emperor Chūai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q179971}})
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} ({{ill|Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|息長帯日売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okitsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神功皇后|1=Empress Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232803}}) | shakaku=Former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | established={{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Taihō 3|1=Taiho}} 3 ({{ill|703|en|703|WD=Q23500|lt=703|1=703}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kure, Hiroshima|en|Kure, Hiroshima|WD=Q223439|lt=Kure|1=Kure, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|WD=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province|1=Buzen Province}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Q11447402|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Q11535100|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19|1=1886}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16|1=1941}} (1941), and in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=Showa 32|1=1957}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Q11447402|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447402}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Q11535100|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11535100}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|1886|en|1886|WD=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19|1=1886}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=Showa 16|1=1941}} (1941), and in {{ill|1957|en|1957|WD=Q5311|lt=Showa 32|1=1957}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
