{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} | established={{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|qid=Q423370|lt=Taihō 3|1=Taiho}} 3 ({{ill|703|en|703|qid=Q23500|lt=703|1=703}}) | honden={{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_names=Hachiman-sama | fudasyo_etc= | main_festival=Second Sunday in October, including the preceding Saturday and Sunday | shinji= }} '''Kameyama Shrine''' (亀山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kure, Hiroshima|en|Kure, Hiroshima|qid=Q223439|lt=Kure|1=Kure, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. The former {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代Rank制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} classification was a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and it is currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is revered as the general {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|qid=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} of the old Kure city area. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}}, namely Hondawakeno Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), Oshihomimi no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}), and Okitsuhime no Mikoto ({{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}). File:亀山神社 二の鳥居.jpg|二の鳥居 == History == The shrine traces its origins to the {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} that was initially enshrined in {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} in {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|qid=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province|1=Buzen Province}}. It is said that the deity was relocated through {{ill|Himeshima|ja|姫島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeshima|1=Q11447402|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11447402}} in {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}, and {{ill|Togihara|ja|栃原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Togihara|1=Q11535100|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11535100}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|qid=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, before finally being enshrined at Kameyama (Irifuneyama) in Miyahara Village on August 15, Taihō 3 (703). The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|1886|en|1886|qid=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19|1=1886}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|1941|en|1941|qid=Q5231|lt=Showa 16|1=1941}} (1941), and in {{ill|1957|en|1957|qid=Q5311|lt=Showa 32|1=1957}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:亀山神社_(呉市清水)]] {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} [[ja:亀山神社 (呉市清水)]] {{wikidata link|Q11370879}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine has been known by various names, including Ōjō-no-miya, Ōyabe Hime Shrine, Ōtake Hime Shrine, Hime Shima Shrine, and Suzune-no-miya, but it was commonly referred to as "Hachiman-sama." In {{ill|1886|en|1886|qid=Q7822|lt=Meiji 19|1=1886}} (1886), the shrine was relocated to its current location to facilitate the establishment of the Kure Naval District. The shrine was elevated to the Rank of prefectural shrine in {{ill|1941|en|1941|qid=Q5231|lt=Showa 16|1=1941}} (1941), and in {{ill|1957|en|1957|qid=Q5311|lt=Showa 32|1=1957}} (1957), it was designated as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Irifuneyama Park|ja|入船山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irifuneyama Park|1=Irifuneyama Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11389023}} == External Links == * [http://www.kameyama-jinja.com Kameyama Shrine] (Official website) * [http://www.facebook.com/Kameyama.Jinja Kameyama Shrine Facebook] (Official) {{神道 横}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|version=102010882|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kameyama-jinja Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11370879|co|commons:Category:Kameyama-jinja Shrine|ja|亀山神社 (呉市清水)|ja|亀山神社_(呉市清水)}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
